paper_title: Hamelia patens Pollination and Enzyme Activity Study

methods.description: This method involves emasculation and controlled pollination of two varieties of Hamelia patens to study enzyme activity in different pollination scenarios.

steps.description: Early in the morning, a day before controlled pollination, mature flower buds were emasculated and bagged with butter paper bags at 'preballoon' stage, 18-24 h before pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: emasculate and bag flower buds for controlled pollination

steps.description: For self-pollination, flower bud at the 'balloon' stage was pollinated by dusting the pollen grains from the same flower on the surface of the stigma and bagged.
steps.searchable_summary: perform self-pollination by dusting pollen on stigma

steps.description: In the cross pollination, variety patens was considered as female plant and variety glabra considered as male plant.
steps.searchable_summary: perform cross-pollination between two plant varieties

steps.description: Following samples were collected for the test of isozymes.

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of enzyme extracts from pistil samples for enzyme activity assays.

steps.description: Part of the pistil without ovary was collected and frozen in -40°C.
Sample weighing about 450 mg were homogenized with 9 ml of cold 5% KCl (w/v) using a pre-chilled mortar and pestle.
The homogenate was centrifuged at 0 ± 2°C at 10,000 rpm for 10 min.
The clear supernatant was used directly for the assay of enzyme activities and estimation of protein.
steps.searchable_summary: use supernatant for enzyme activity assay

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data from enzyme activity assays and pollination experiments.

steps.description: The data were collected from five sets of experiments and were subjected to analysis of standard deviation.
